text:
Deep Learning is an area of digital image processing that focuses on different techniques for image denoising, image enhancement, image segmentation, feature extraction and image classification. Images contain a large amount of information that must be processed, analyzed and understood through image analysis in order to accomplish various tasks. This processing requires execution of numerous steps and various image processing techniques. Collectively these techniques deal with different aspects of image analysis and are extremely useful in medical imaging, remote sensing and robotic vision applications. General image processing methods include the use of handcrafted algorithms and heuristics. They do not learn, instead they work on the existing data and are unable to obtain the information hidden within the data. They are able to process only those data that they have learned. Deep Learning models learn various abstractions within different layers from the input data and are able to automatically learn the significant features from the data that might be missed with other image processing methods.

Denoising DnCNNs (Deep Neural Network) named as Self2Self NN, DFT-Net (Deep Filter Bank in Transform domain), MPR-CNN etc. are designed to suppress noise and data augmentation, model selection are to be done with care. Image enhancement techniques namely, RetinexDIP (Deep Image Prior) and LE-net have shown improved image quality and there is a scope for understanding real world scene authenticity. Several segmentation techniques namely, PSPNet (Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition), Mask R-CNN have shown precision in identifying the objects and still there are challenges like objects having overlaps and multi-condition image capture. Many feature extraction techniques like CNN, hybrid model namely, HLF-DIP have shown successful AI based feature identification with the trade-off for interpretability. Classification models ranging from Residual Networks to CNN-LSTMs have shown precise classification from various databases. This article reviews and discusses various image processing applications and highlights the pros and cons of several models with respect to its application in real time.
